schools.summary: The record of the kings smitten east of Jordan: 'now these are the kings of the land, which the children of Israel smote, and possessed their land on the other side Jordan toward the rising of the sun, from the river Arnon unto mount Hermon, and all the plain on the east' — the chapter records the kings defeated and the lands possessed east of Jordan (the now these are the kings of the land, which the children of Israel smote, the kings smitten, the and possessed their land on the other side Jordan toward the rising of the sun, from the river Arnon unto mount Hermon, and all the plain on the east, the lands possessed east of Jordan, the figure, the record of the victories, the kings smitten and the lands possessed, the memorial of God's works, the truth that the conquests were recorded as a witness of God's faithfulness, the figure of the remembrance of God's mighty works, the victories told, 'I will remember the works of the LORD,' Psalm 77:11, the kings smitten east of Jordan, the inheritance possessed, the record of the conquest, vv.7); the kings smitten by Israel recorded (the record, Psalm 77:11) — the kings (smitten); 'possessed their land on the other side Jordan... from the river Arnon unto mount Hermon' (the lands possessed east of Jordan, the inheritance, the figure, the remembrance of God's mighty works, the providence, the record, Sihon king of the Amorites to follow, vv.2) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

schools.summary: Sihon king of the Amorites and the extent of his realm: 'Sihon king of the Amorites, who dwelt in Heshbon, and ruled from Aroer, which is upon the bank of the river Arnon, and from the middle of the river, and from half Gilead, even unto the river Jabbok, which is the border of the children of Ammon' — Sihon, the Amorite king of Heshbon, ruled a wide realm east of Jordan (the Sihon king of the Amorites, who dwelt in Heshbon, Sihon of Heshbon, the and ruled from Aroer, which is upon the bank of the river Arnon, and from the middle of the river, and from half Gilead, even unto the river Jabbok, the extent of his realm, the figure, the great king Sihon, who refused Israel passage and fell, his realm given to Israel, the truth that Sihon, the first great foe east of Jordan, was smitten and his land possessed, the figure of the proud king brought low and his realm given to God's people, 'Sihon king of the Amorites... for his mercy endureth for ever,' Psalm 136:19, the king and his realm taken, the first conquest east of Jordan, vv.1); Sihon, the Amorite king of Heshbon (the king, Psalm 136:19) — the king (Sihon of Heshbon); 'ruled from Aroer... even unto the river Jabbok' (the extent of Sihon's realm, the wide kingdom, the figure, the proud king brought low and his realm given, the providence, the realm, the plain to the sea of Chinneroth to follow, vv.3) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: Sihon king of the Amorites, who dwelt in Heshbon, and ruled from Aroer, which is upon the bank of the river Arnon, and from the middle of the river, and from half Gilead, even unto the river Jabbok, which is the border of the children of Ammon
Sihon king of the Amorites and the extent of his realm
Sihon, the Amorite king of Heshbon, who ruled a wide realm and refused Israel passage

schools.summary: The bounds of Sihon's realm to the salt sea: 'and from the plain to the sea of Chinneroth on the east, and unto the sea of the plain, even the salt sea on the east, the way to Bethjeshimoth; and from the south, under Ashdothpisgah' — the realm of Sihon reached from the sea of Chinneroth to the salt sea (the and from the plain to the sea of Chinneroth on the east, to the sea of Chinneroth, the and unto the sea of the plain, even the salt sea on the east, the way to Bethjeshimoth; and from the south, under Ashdothpisgah, to the salt sea, the figure, the bounds of the realm given to Israel, the wide inheritance, the truth that the whole realm of Sihon was possessed, the land given, the figure of the inheritance fully bounded and possessed, the gift complete, the bounds of the conquest east of Jordan, the realm given, vv.2); the realm reaching to the sea of Chinneroth (the bound) — the bound (to Chinneroth); 'unto the sea of the plain, even the salt sea... under Ashdothpisgah' (the realm to the salt sea, the inheritance, the figure, the inheritance fully bounded and possessed, the providence, the bounds, the realm of Og king of Bashan to follow, vv.4) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And from the plain to the sea of Chinneroth on the east, and unto the sea of the plain, even the salt sea on the east, the way to Bethjeshimoth; and from the south, under Ashdothpisgah

schools.summary: Og king of Bashan, of the remnant of the giants: 'and the coast of Og king of Bashan, which was of the remnant of the giants, that dwelt at Ashtaroth and at Edrei' — Og, the giant king of Bashan, was smitten and his land possessed (the and the coast of Og king of Bashan, which was of the remnant of the giants, Og of the giants, the that dwelt at Ashtaroth and at Edrei, his cities, the figure, the giant king Og, of the remnant of the Rephaim, smitten by Israel, the truth that even the giant king fell before God's people, his realm given, the figure of the great and terrible foe overcome by God's power, the giant brought low, 'Og the king of Bashan: for his mercy endureth for ever,' Psalm 136:20, the giant king fallen, the second great conquest east of Jordan, vv.2); Og, of the remnant of the giants (the giant king, Psalm 136:20) — the giant (Og of Bashan); 'that dwelt at Ashtaroth and at Edrei' (Og's royal cities, the realm, the figure, the great foe overcome, the providence, the giant, the extent of Og's realm to follow, vv.5) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

schools.summary: The extent of Og's realm over Bashan and half Gilead: 'and reigned in mount Hermon, and in Salcah, and in all Bashan, unto the border of the Geshurites and the Maachathites, and half Gilead, the border of Sihon king of Heshbon' — Og's realm reached over Bashan to the border of Sihon's (the and reigned in mount Hermon, and in Salcah, and in all Bashan, Og's realm over Bashan, the unto the border of the Geshurites and the Maachathites, and half Gilead, the border of Sihon king of Heshbon, to the border of Sihon, the figure, the wide realm of Og given to Israel, the two kingdoms east of Jordan possessed, the truth that the realms of Sihon and Og together were given to Israel, the inheritance east of Jordan, the figure of the two great kingdoms overcome and given, the inheritance possessed, the realm of Og taken, the conquest east of Jordan complete, vv.4); Og reigning over Bashan (the realm) — the realm (over Bashan); 'unto the border of the Geshurites and the Maachathites, and half Gilead, the border of Sihon' (Og's realm to the border of Sihon's, the two realms, the figure, the two great kingdoms overcome and given, the providence, the realm, Moses giving the land to the two and a half tribes to follow, vv.6) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

schools.summary: The record of the kings Joshua smote west of Jordan and the land given to the tribes: 'and these are the kings of the country which Joshua and the children of Israel smote on this side Jordan on the west, from Baalgad in the valley of Lebanon even unto the mount Halak, that goeth up to Seir; which Joshua gave unto the tribes of Israel for a possession according to their divisions' — the inheritance given — 'and these are the kings of the country which Joshua and the children of Israel smote on this side Jordan on the west, from Baalgad in the valley of Lebanon even unto the mount Halak... which Joshua gave unto the tribes of Israel for a possession according to their divisions': Joshua smote the kings west of Jordan and gave their land to the tribes for an inheritance (the witnesses noting the significance — the faithfulness of God in giving his people the land he promised, the kings smitten and the inheritance distributed, the whole land west of Jordan, from Lebanon to Seir, conquered and given to the tribes by their divisions, the lesson — that God keeps his covenant and gives his people their inheritance, the conquest the proof of his faithfulness, 'the LORD gave unto Israel all the land which he sware to give unto their fathers... there failed not ought of any good thing,' Joshua 21:43-45, the truth that the inheritance was God's gift, distributed to each tribe by lot, the land possessed as promised, 'for ye shall inherit the land,' Numbers 33:54, the figure — the inheritance given to God's people, fulfilled in the eternal inheritance of the saints, 'an inheritance incorruptible, and undefiled... reserved in heaven for you,' 1 Peter 1:4, the truth that God brings his people into their inheritance and divides it among them, each having his portion, the figure of the heavenly inheritance distributed to the saints, each his place, the call to trust the faithful God who gives the promised inheritance, the assurance that not one of God's promises fails, the figure of the faithfulness of God in giving his people the promised inheritance); the record of the kings Joshua smote from Lebanon to Seir, the whole land west of Jordan conquered (the conquest, Joshua 21:43-45) — the kings (smitten west of Jordan); 'which Joshua gave unto the tribes of Israel for a possession according to their divisions' (the land given to the tribes by their divisions, the inheritance distributed as God promised, a figure of the eternal inheritance of the saints, the inheritance possessed, Numbers 33:54; 1 Peter 1:4) — the figure (the heavenly inheritance distributed to the saints, the providence, the inheritance, the list of the thirty-one kings to follow, vv.9-24) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does the record of the conquered kings and the divided land establish?

sides.position: The faithfulness of God in giving his people the promised inheritance - the kings smitten and the land distributed to each tribe by their divisions, God keeping his covenant and failing in no good thing he had spoken (Joshua 21:43-45; Numbers 33:54), a figure of the eternal inheritance of the saints, incorruptible and reserved in heaven (1 Peter 1:4)

themes: And these are the kings of the country which Joshua and the children of Israel smote on this side Jordan on the west, from Baalgad in the valley of Lebanon even unto the mount Halak, that goeth up to Seir; which Joshua gave unto the tribes of Israel for a possession according to their divisions
the record of the kings Joshua smote west of Jordan and the land given to the tribes (the crux of the faithfulness of God in giving his people the promised inheritance)
the faithfulness of God in giving his people the land he promised, the kings smitten and the inheritance distributed to each tribe by their divisions, God keeping his covenant
a figure of the eternal inheritance of the saints, incorruptible and reserved in heaven (Joshua 21:43-45; Numbers 33:54; 1 Peter 1:4)

the roll continuing with Taanach and Megiddo, in the plain of Esdraelon
each numbered in the record of God's victories

schools.summary: The list continuing with the kings of Kedesh and Jokneam: 'the king of Kedesh, one; the king of Jokneam of Carmel, one' — the roll continues with Kedesh and Jokneam of Carmel (the the king of Kedesh, one, Kedesh, the the king of Jokneam of Carmel, one, Jokneam, the figure, the kings numbered, the victories told, the truth that Kedesh, a city of refuge, and Jokneam by Carmel fell and are numbered, the figure of the record that counts God's victories one by one, the kings numbered, the roll continued, vv.21); the king of Kedesh, later a city of refuge (the roll) — Kedesh (one); 'the king of Jokneam of Carmel, one' (the king of Jokneam by Carmel, the roll, the figure, the record that counts God's victories, the providence, the kings, Dor and the nations of Gilgal to follow, vv.23) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: The king of Kedesh, one; the king of Jokneam of Carmel, one
the list continuing with the kings of Kedesh and Jokneam
the roll continuing with Kedesh, later a city of refuge, and Jokneam by Carmel
each numbered in the record of God's victories

schools.summary: The list continuing with the kings of Dor and the nations of Gilgal: 'the king of Dor in the coast of Dor, one; the king of the nations of Gilgal, one' — the roll continues with Dor on the coast and the king of the nations of Gilgal (the the king of Dor in the coast of Dor, one, Dor, the the king of the nations of Gilgal, one, the nations of Gilgal, the figure, the kings of the coast and the mingled nations numbered, the truth that Dor, on the coast, and the king of the confederate nations of Gilgal fell and are numbered, the figure of the record of God's victories over the nations, each told, the kings numbered, the roll near its end, vv.22); the king of Dor on the coast (the roll) — Dor (one); 'the king of the nations of Gilgal, one' (the king of the confederate nations of Gilgal, the roll, the figure, the record of God's victories over the nations, the providence, the kings, Tirzah and the sum of thirty-one to follow, vv.24) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: The king of Dor in the coast of Dor, one; the king of the nations of Gilgal, one
the list continuing with the kings of Dor and the nations of Gilgal
the roll continuing with Dor on the coast and the king of the confederate nations of Gilgal
each numbered in the record of God's victories

schools.summary: The close of the list, the king of Tirzah and the sum of all the kings, thirty and one: 'the king of Tirzah, one: all the kings thirty and one' — the full tally — 'the king of Tirzah, one: all the kings thirty and one': the roll closes with Tirzah, and the whole number of conquered kings is summed, thirty and one (the witnesses noting the significance — the completeness of the conquest and of God's faithfulness, the full number of the kings subdued set down as a memorial, the thirty-one kings, the whole power of Canaan broken, the victory entire and the record complete, the lesson — that God gave his people a full and complete victory, every king numbered, none of his promises failing, the tally a witness that he did all he said, 'there failed not ought of any good thing which the LORD had spoken unto the house of Israel; all came to pass,' Joshua 21:45, the truth that God's works are to be numbered and remembered, the mercies told one by one, 'who can utter the mighty acts of the LORD? who can shew forth all his praise?' Psalm 106:2, the figure — the full record of God's victories, the complete triumph over the powers arrayed against his people, a figure of the final and total victory of Christ over all his enemies, 'he must reign, till he hath put all enemies under his feet,' 1 Corinthians 15:25, the truth that not one of God's foes is left unsubdued, the conquest entire, the figure of the complete and final victory of God over all the powers of evil, the call to remember and number God's mercies, the assurance that God finishes the work he begins, the figure of the completeness of God's victory and the full record of his faithfulness); the roll closing with the king of Tirzah, the last of the conquered kings named (the record complete, Psalm 106:2) — the last king (Tirzah); 'all the kings thirty and one' (the whole number summed, thirty and one kings, the completeness of the conquest and of God's faithfulness, none of his promises failing, a figure of the final and total victory of Christ over all his foes, the complete victory, Joshua 21:45; 1 Corinthians 15:25) — the figure (the complete and final victory of God over the powers of evil, the providence, the sum, the division of the land to follow, ch.13) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does the sum 'all the kings thirty and one' establish?

sides.position: The completeness of God's victory and the full record of his faithfulness - the whole power of Canaan broken and every king numbered, God failing in no good thing he had spoken (Joshua 21:45), his mighty acts to be numbered and remembered (Psalm 106:2), a figure of the final and total victory of Christ over all his enemies, who must reign till all are put under his feet (1 Corinthians 15:25)

themes: The king of Tirzah, one: all the kings thirty and one
the close of the list, the king of Tirzah and the sum of all the kings, thirty and one (the crux of the completeness of God's victory and the full record of his faithfulness)
the completeness of the conquest and of God's faithfulness, the whole power of Canaan broken and every king numbered, God failing in no good thing he had spoken
a figure of the final and total victory of Christ over all his enemies, who must reign till all are put under his feet (Joshua 21:45; Psalm 106:2; 1 Corinthians 15:25)
